text: I've visited this place since January 2025 and it's just perfect! I like all the food here and the baristas make amazing coffee and cold non-alcoholic drinks too. Sometimes I go here for some interesting events. That's a really cozy place with friendly people.

Went for a late coffee and ordered the bline with cream cheese and jam.
Two latte coffees one large and one medium and also a cinnamon bun with almond creme.
The coffee was tasty and the cinnamon bun was great. The bline was tasty with the two fillings.

Good coffee and cake, however for such a small place the atmosphere on the day i visited was too much: a lady loudly speaking, prob having a zoom call was dominating the space like we are in her office, which we are not. It was uncomfortable and I planned to sit down and relax but had to finish quickly my cake and leave.
I get it, people can work from cafes, no issues, but I think it was already a universal truth to either keep others in mind having a call or for the call use dedicated spaces like co-working. Unfortunately, it appears that the lady was a friend of people managing / working at the place so don't think my feedback will be taken well :)
However, im sure people would enjoy delicious coffee and food you have more if the were enjoying the space around them, too.

I keep coming back to this place for its excellent specialty coffee, nice food (check out their gluten-free syrniki) and friendly staff. Most importantly for Buenos Aires is their consistently high quality of coffee which is quite rare here. Highly recommended!

Birdy Birds is such a cute cafe. The barista who was working when I visited was so incredibly nice! The coffee was good. I also got a slice of lemon poppyseed bread which was ok. It’s very small but cozy and a nice spot to grab a coffee with a friend.

The coffee here was pretty good and the service is great. It’s also a cute place to sit inside and has a couple tables outside. I also had a Chipa sandwich and it was very dry. I would not recommend that part of the food menu and it turned me off enough. I probably wouldn’t go back. It was also a little bit more expensive than other cafés in the area, which is probably why I was the only one in there.

I have been to every coffee shop in a two block radius and this is my favorite! The coffee and food are really good. The place always smells like fresh baked goods. They have indoor and outdoor seating. They are pet friendly. Good WiFi. Great, friendly staff!

Great service & Coffee. Happy it was nearby our place. We got the flat white, mocaccino, and passion fruit cheesecake. The coffee was made well very smooth, cheesecake was on the thicker dense side but well balance of tart & sweet.

Nice seating area to enjoy your coffee.

Grate coffee and pastries
In these days people make sour coffee. This place doesn't which is great
Lovely place for breakfast. Everything is fresh and coffee was excellent. Well looked after by Damien and the other staff.

Went here on a Friday night with friends (reservation for 4, highly recommend booking ahead). We got there around 9pm and they had live music playing almost the whole night — such a fun vibe. The interior design and overall atmosphere really give you that speakeasy feel, stylish and unique.

We ordered the appetizer “tower” to share (mini burgers and snacks, solid but nothing mind-blowing) and a couple of oyster plates (just okay). Drinks were great, though most leaned on the stronger/earthy side — not many sweet cocktail options if that’s your thing. Still, the menu is creative and well-curated.

Price-wise, it adds up quickly — with the apps, oysters, and a couple drinks each, the bill was over $300 for four people. Definitely on the expensive side, but it felt worth it for the experience.

Service was attentive without being pushy. They checked in often at first, but later gave us space to relax and enjoy, which I really appreciated — no rushing to finish or leave.

Overall, a great place for a chill but classy evening with friends. Would absolutely recommend if you’re looking for a fun speakeasy experience in Manhattan.

What is there to tell about Mme George except that the program is as colorful as the staff. Chris was one of the bar team tonight taking care of us. Cracking jokes, playing pseudo therapist & moderator slash master of ceremonies.

In the best way, Chris (and the rest of the team) are very direct & very real reason to visit. Between my wife & I visiting from upstate NY, we met a couple visiting from BC, Canada who were thoroughly entertained with the interactions.

Drinks can be forgotten but service is the most memorable part of the guest experience.

Keep it up y'all.

With love,
Anton & Lisa
Lone Wolf Kingston, NY

Great ambiance and vibe! Especially good for couples and small-medium groups! Amazing bartenders and staff!
If, like me, you’re not big fan of sweet drinks, try 32 Cans and Mary’s Interlude!! Guaranteed!
Also, if you are looking for recommendations or have any questions, look for Miranda, superb bartender—def knows what she is doing and has THE best recommendations!! Inna is also amazing, friendly and knowledgable, runs everything like a piece of cake.

This one of those places that actually delivers on the word “lounge”.

The space is stunning, dim and stylish in a way that feels grown-up, not try-hard. The service was a big part of why the night worked: present and professional, but never pushy. No one doing the annoying “everything good?” drive-by every two minutes or nudging you to reorder.

The music, though, is the reason to go. It was genuinely amazing and set the whole tone. The room feels alive without being chaotic, and the overall atmosphere is just… right.

Food was okay. The meatballs and the bread that came with them leaned too salty for my taste, and nothing we tried was the standout compared to the rest of the experience.

Bottom line: go for the vibe and the music, stay because the place makes you want to linger.

quaint, cozy and quiet. can cause one to be in a state of somnolent. my friend rates the matcha as 3.8. seating space provided with people quietly working on their laptops. good place to study and work.

Restroom available
Wifi available
Water available

This bar has personality. The interior is simultaneously kitschy and classic. You enter into a plain dark room, proceed down a fluorescently lit stairwell decorated like a subway station, and finally into a sumptuous space with velvet seating and a well stocked and well stacked bar. The drinks are creative and evidently wrought and worthy of their loftier price. The food also trades on novelty, although with slightly worse execution than the drinks and atmosphere.

We had a lovely brunch at this cute cafe in the Palermo Hollywood area. The vibe was perfect. We had an avocado toast, quinoa bowl with chicken and a chicken burger, orange juice and coffee. It was a fresh and filling meal and felt very satisfied after consumption.
The service was also great and the restaurant was green and beautiful.

Amazing coffee spot in Palermo! The food options are excellent, with a big focus on fresh, all-natural ingredients. Everything tastes homemade and thoughtfully prepared, from the pastries to the savory dishes. Great coffee, healthy choices, and a cozy atmosphere that makes you want to stay longer. Highly recommended if you’re looking for quality food and good vibes in the neighborhood!

A Must-Visit Day Dining Spot in Buenos Aires! 5 Stars Across the Board

I simply cannot stress enough how much I recommend Rita Specialty Coffee! I've been here for breakfast, brunch, and lunch, and every single dish is stunning. The variety and quality are consistently excellent.

Personally, I highly recommend the Avocado Toast, the Pollito de la Casa, and the Ibérico Sandwich—they are all phenomenal. Their specialty coffee is amazing, and the fresh fruit juices are equally refreshing.

Beyond the food, the service makes this place shine. Cristian was our server, and he was incredible—always attentive, incredibly kind, and brought such a good vibe to our experience.

This is truly the perfect place for day dining in Buenos Aires. A definite must! Go, you won't regret it.

I tried menú almuerzo and it was the best decision of that day. They have many healthy options for lunch or delicious sweet treats and their coffee - perfect temperature, taste and size. I’ll be back for sure :)

Great thanks  for Valentina great english,  very nice and wonderful person. In fact "Rita" is the beautiful place and atmospheric and very cozy. Here you can find real speciallyty coffee with tasty dessert

One of the best places for breakfast or brunch in Buenos Aires, it’s so cozy and nice, people are great, location is good. There are not that much place but waiting worth it definitely. Sandwiches and toasts are great! I would like to see more new vegetarian options in the menu.

Walked down here after getting some coffee at Oli . I wasn’t satisfied with my coffee there and this spot looked interesting. With its yellow painted building and people sitting outside - I gave it a shot and thank goodness I did - they have excellent coffee , the taste is great, the service is great - I recommend ! Had the magic and flat white - both were good ! 👍

I'm very impressed with the service of this place. We ordered the Mandale Fruta, iced latte and frappe. The freshness of the food and drink was superb. The staff were very patient with us when placing the orders since we don't speak English. Paula was very helpful thoughout our visit. 10/10 all around

Vive Cafe has quickly become my favorite cafe in Palermo. The coffee is excellent, the pastries are consistently great, and the staff are so welcoming and friendly. The coffee beans are also great quality and they are happy to grind them for you.
Great coffee! Pleasant spot to hang out and work for awhile. Recommend the tonic coffee!

I’m so pleased I found this place while traveling in Argentina. I love Japan as it is among my favourite countries and finding a little piece of Japan anywhere in the world is like finding a gem. They sell Japanese sandwiches and cakes. But for me Japan is in a real proper matcha. And this place has it 👌🏻✨💚
I enjoyed it first time and then returned after my Patagonia trip.
Arigatō! 🍃

Amazing coffee and an incredible matcha cookie — both were seriously top-notch. One of the best coffees I’ve had in Buenos Aires. Cozy place, super friendly staff, and a perfect spot for a little break. Highly recommended!

If you are coffeeholic like I am with the thing for speciality coffee - then it’s your place!
We struggle a bit to find a proper coffee roastery / coffee shop that serves flatwhite and ideally with oat milk. Here they do!
Great coffee, welcoming English speaking staff and modern trendy vibes!
Loved this place! The vanilla matcha latte was amazing. Prices are fair and the vibe + staff are awesome. Definitely one of those spots I always enjoy coming back to. Totally recommend.
I’m very picky with matcha, and this matcha iced latte with almond milk exceeded my expectations! Loved it, definitely recommended.

Their fries needed a little bit more salt, but other than that the atmosphere and drinks were great!

Also the prices were good, and their two for ones are also a good perk of going here.

Can't say anything about the food, but the Caipi Limas were excellent!
Special thanks to my great waiter Gonzalo!!!
Really great location, fantastic music selection....

Sote Coffee Roasters is an awesome spot for high-quality coffee. The brews are smooth, rich, and full of flavor — you can really taste the care they put into roasting.
Great desserts options from home made cakes and cookies.

The atmosphere is cozy and modern, with good space to relax or work. Staff are welcoming and knowledgeable, always happy to recommend something new to try.
Definitely one of my favorite coffee stops for both great drinks and great vibes!

We love this place.  It’s a great place to look over the marina in Poulsbo WA.  Food is typical fried bar food, but was enjoyable both times of our visit.  We love the Marina view and the friendly service. We met some locals this year and it was fun to chat with them and enjoyed not feeling out of place being a gay older couple.  We appreciate the friendly staff service and hospitality.  Plenty of beer and adult libations served.  The food is typical fried bar food, but seemed a bit above the usual dive bar.  Can’t complain about the vibe or the wonderful views. Can’t complain about the menu or the staff. Can’t complain about the location or the wonderful view. So this place is a wonderful spot to enjoy an afternoon. Definitely recommend to anyone looking to enjoy the day near the water overlooking the Poulsbo Waterfront.

First time in Argentina and in this place and it was a 10/10 experience. Bianca was our waitress and she was very lovely. Great service and made sure we were taken care of and our drinks were never empty! We had a great time and will definitely be back!

I stopped here for a quick but nice stopover with a friend from my hostel. We had some drinks and felt very welcome, Eros the waiter was speaking English and Español and was very attentive. This is a nice place to hang out and enjoy drinks at the river. I recommend!
Cappuccino was delicious. Very well done. Pastries were great - the fruit tart was very rich. Nice place to sit outside and relax.

I finally found a cafe in Beaunos Aires that serves Melbourne style espresso. I know, I sound like a spoilt brat, but can you blame me? 😁
They also have a small selection of pastries to enjoy. Staff is super friendly and helpful as well, what more can you ask for?
On a leafy road opposite a park, they have outdoor seating only.

The people are kind and the food is really good. We ate “pan de queijo” and it was delicious.
There are few tables to sit but there is a nice plaza just across the street.
Best coffee and service. Close to parks to sit and enjoy your coffee too.
adorable take away shop with delicious espresso drinks and friendly service. close to Marriott and overlooks a nice park. was a morning highlight of my trip.
Great coffee, ordering for almost a year already
Great coffee

Excellent coffee and reasonable prices. ☕

They have a variety of coffees. Great presentation and service. 💯

Keep it up! 👏🏻

Highly recommended. We'll keep buying! 🛒

The product just arrived. I bought the tasting box because I got my first espresso machine, and I chose it because I loved the idea. I don't know much about coffee, and it's fantastic for being able to try different coffee options and choose the one I like best for a future purchase. 10/10 for everything, and the shipping was super fast.
Muy buena atención digital, ya sea por página web, redes y WhatsApp. El producto es de muy buena calidad!! Probé el combo carioca y me encantó! Brindando variedad y gusto en el café.

Very nice place to start your morning with a good coffee and pastry. The choice of pastries is limited but they have nice combo deals, for example a coffee with croissant or a coffee with two medialunas. And it was one of the few places I found in BA that offers a variety of plant based milk 🌿👍 The service was excellent, super friendly, attentive and quick. Appreciated the complimentary glass of water. Nice seating in a semi-outdoor bar-type setting from where you can watch the world go by on the busy avenue in front. Free WiFi, just note that there is no toilet.

Amazing! Great service, coffee and pastries are one the best what I've experienced in Buenos Aires.  Thank you so much! I'll miss this place! Also it's pet friendly, and my little boy got nice time, water and attention😁
Very good coffee, location and service.  Friendly barista.
I am a regular customern excellent attention !! It s the best barista coffee in all around the Avenida Corrientes!!
Great place to grab a quality coffee made to perfection by lovely helpful staff, and that’s coming from Australians who care much about their coffee. We’ll be back soon

A very cozy, welcoming spot with a genuinely homey feel. Small tables, comfortable chairs, and even a little couch that makes you want to stay longer than planned.

The coffee is done properly, smooth and well balanced, and the pastries are fresh and delicious. The service is genuinely friendly and attentive without hovering, which is rarer than it should be.

This place feels personal and relaxed, like a neighborhood café that actually cares. Easy five stars.

SOTE Coffee Roasters in NYC was an incredible experience from start to finish — the space felt intentional, calm, and genuinely crafted for people who love great coffee. The barista took time to walk me through their espresso options, and I ended up grabbing a bag of beans that pulled beautifully with rich crema and a smooth, balanced finish. If you care about quality roasting and thoughtful hospitality, SOTE absolutely delivers.

Highly recommend for coffee lovers in UWS ☕
This coffee shop has such a lovely atmosphere — it’s small, cozy, and super welcoming. You can smell the coffee from the moment you walk in. The drinks taste great, nicely bold and perfectly balanced — exactly my kind of coffee.

The owner and staff are incredibly friendly and make you feel comfortable right away. It’s a perfect spot whether you want a quick grab & go or to sit and chill with friends 🤍

Best part… it’s dog-friendly too! 🐶✨ Love this place so much.

Incredible coffee, food, staff and environment! Genuinely one of my favourite spots if not the favourite spot in Buenos Aries. The staff are genuinely so kind and bring their passion into the space. Cannot recommend enough! We grabbed coffees, breakfast and the pastries and all were WOW!! If you are looking for an incredible coffee shop that serves the best pastries and breakfast along with the coffee, look no further!

We loved this coffee shop! It is a nice owner-operated place located on a lovely cobblestone street in Palermo, with both indoor and outdoor seating options. The ambience is very relaxed and peaceful. The owner is such a nice guy and takes great pride in his work. His dedication really shows through in the quality of the coffee.

On top of the coffee, they also bake many delicious products that they personally make on-site such as croissants, cinnamon rolls, and cookies. We honestly did not have anything here that we didn’t love. The croissant was my personal favorite.

I just can’t say enough about how great this place was. We literally went here everyday of our trip. Thank you so much and keep up the great work!

Worst matcha I’ve had in a while. Perhaps it was not the best option to order, but their matcha latte was essentially milk. You can see this in the photos which are purely white. The service was friendly though
Cozy spot on the corner of a lovely street in Palermo Hollywood. The coffee was great and service was even better. Thomas was so friendly
On my first trip to Buenos Aires, this was the first spot where I genuinely felt welcomed. Incredible coffee and even better service. Huge thanks to Tomas for going above and beyond!

Backroom Bar was an amazing find. We came here after it was recommended for an espresso martini, and it absolutely lived up to the hype. We ended up trying several other cocktails as well, and every single one was excellent—creative, well-balanced, and expertly made. The food was just as impressive and complemented the drinks perfectly. Overall, it was a fantastic experience, and I would definitely come back here again on a future visit to Buenos Aires.

The place itself is cozy with good music! I stayed in the bar, but I can’t complain. The waiters treated me very well. It was a Friday night and we were passing by, it was crowded, good music and we decided to give it a try. There were no tables available, that's why we stayed in the bar. The drink I had chosen was great, and all the food I ordered was delicious! I highly recommend it for anyone who is visiting Buenos Aires or around. Overall, great place to enjoy some drinks and eat good food!

We spent a wonderful evening at Backroom Bar in Buenos Aires and it quickly became one of our favourite spots in the city. Tucked away behind a discreet entrance, the place opens up into a beautiful interior courtyard that feels like a hidden oasis in the middle of the city. It’s the perfect setting to unwind, especially at the start of the evening.

We ordered a couple of cocktails and they were truly outstanding, well balanced, creative, and clearly crafted with a lot of attention to detail. The menu offers a great mix of classics and signature drinks, and everything we tried was impressive. The atmosphere is elegant yet relaxed, making it an ideal place for an apéritif before dinner.

The service was warm and attentive without being intrusive, which added to the overall charm of the experience. Whether you’re looking for a romantic spot, a place to meet friends, or simply somewhere to enjoy high-quality cocktails in a gorgeous setting, Backroom Bar is absolutely worth a visit.

Behind a quiet door in SoHo, the Backroom Bar is delivering uncommonly great cocktails. We’ve tried many of the best bars in town but believe what we had today with Bruno and Carina as our bartenders was especially inspiring, inventive and delicious! I highly recommend trying it.

This place was awesome. Their cocktails are some of the best I’ve tried. We tried the strawberry cheesecake, caminito, and several others. However, the service was the best part. Sandro let me borrow his charger, even cleaned my mate gourd out, was super friendly. My wife ordered a drink she didn’t like and they even took it off the bill after we insisted that we would pay. They only wanted to know why she didn’t like it so that they could make it better. We also had a chance to listen to live jazz while we’re there, which was awesome too. The food looked awesome and we are going back to try the food since it smelled so good.
Best coffee I've had in Buenos Aires. The ham and cheese toasty on olive bread was delicious too. Lovely staff and atmosphere.
The coffee was good, but needs to be hotter!! The latte art was exquisite as well as the Cinnamon roll 😋 small, cute cafe. The sandwich was HUGE, best to share with two people.

This is the best cafe in the entire neighborhood. Excellent coffee & pastries (try the delicious chipa!) But the best part - the extremely friendly employees who greet everyone with a friendly & helpful demeanor (especially the morning crew). I highly recommend. ❤️

Definitely one of the best  coffee spots in Buenos Aires. Delicious coffee, gorgeous ceramic cups and plates. Excellent customer service and a very friendly calm environment. I go almost everyday now.

We came here for coffee, and flatwhite was unfortunately very average(

The prices are ok. You have enough place to sit both inside and outside, for the place of this type. No vegan+glutenfree options in pastries. Though you can find some vegan treats, and sin tacc alfahores

Cute and cozy place with whimsical décor and an amazing vibe! The food was a total surprise for a bar—delicious and tasted like a home-cooked meal. Service was great, and the unexpected belly dance shows made the night even more special.
A must-visit if you’re in Buenos Aires!
Good experience at Verne Club.
Too crowded.

Bestial FlyBar was an utter disappointment. Don’t get me wrong - the view of the city and sunset is amazing and the decor is fun. But beyond that, the bar is not worth the cost. The drinks are just okay and are not special at all except for their containers.  My family got the Homero drink, the Diana cocktail & the Gardel cocktail and their containers were pretty cool. But tempura langonista (starter) had way too much tempura and was overcooked. The ceviche was overly saturated in lime juice and I couldn’t finish it. The passion sushi roll was majority cream cheese with one small shrimp 🍤 mixed in and some crushed walnuts thrown in top. Out of three people, nobody was able to finish their meal due to poorly prepared food and we left still hungry as well as disappointed. My group did get great sunset photos but we also left the restaurant very disappointed - disappointed with the poor quality of the drinks and poor quality of the food, not to mention the high prices of everything. My family had high expectations of Bestial FlyBar and the restaurant was truly awful. I will not be visiting Bestial FlyBar again and I would not suggest or recommend for anyone to visit this restaurant. Again, Bestial FlyBar was an utter disappointment and very underwhelming.

Wow. This place is so quirky and unashamed to be itself and lean into whatever design style this is called. I came here for lunch and I was very surprised to see the red geode cross section floor, leopard upholstered seats, naked silver female robot, and a large number of 3d printed heads used for tiki drinks. Honestly, it blew my mind. I had a very beautifully done salmon steak with a thinly sliced red beet vegetable cake with crispy kale and a parsnip puree. Pleasantly surprised and it actually felt quite modern and elevated. (I also just finished watching 24 in 24 so maybe I was in a different headspace). If you like spicy drinks, then get the Homer. It has a great little kick. Plus... Mmmm... Donuts. The edm music was bumping. Highly recommended for those who want to see a good view and want to go somewhere super unique.

First impression: The venue presents itself like a comic brought to life, with a colorful setting, strong beats, and constant music. The sushi was average, and the cocktails were unremarkable. Overall, the price–performance ratio does not justify the offering. For a sky bar, the view should be a highlight; unfortunately, it falls short of expectations.

Late night sushi menu is mediocre at best, but good drinks and cool vibes. The 15-piece "chefs selection" were rolls that consisted of half cream cheese and half salmon with various toppings. The nirigi is on the dinner menu and not the late night menu. The various heads the drinks are served in were cool and the atmosphere was great. The service not so much.

I’ll start off by saying I’m not a coffee drinker.  But 787 was close, it was cold & had time to spare. So good  I went back two days in a row.

The coquito latte was creamy and perfect — and Jonathan’s recommendation to add the rum coffee beans would’ve been a crime to skip. Absolute upgrade.

Day two I had the horchata — smooth, lightly sweet, and just as delicious.

The inside is bright, welcoming, and such a nice place to linger. Great drinks, even better service. A lovely spot.

An amazing little Puerto Rican Gem in the heart of SOHO. Loved the atmosphere, the coffee was PHENOMENAL! They had different flavored beans imported directly from their farm in Puerto Rico. Jonathan behind the counter was super helpful and informative.

A Delightful Stop with Stellar Service and Spanish Flair

​Five stars! This coffee shop is definitely worth a stop and offers a refreshing change from the usual choices, thanks to its unique Spanish-influenced coffees.

​The welcome and service were truly outstanding. John provided a great experience. His recommendation was spot-on and perfectly aligned with my taste—a clear sign of an attentive and knowledgeable staff member. The cookie was yummy and perfectly moist.

​If you’re looking for high-quality service and a unique twist on your coffee routine, this is the place to be!

Very good and unique coffee choice. They have a variety of flavors, beans and milk types. I took dulce de lece coffee and it was delicious, the coffee is served in a small bag which i found funny and innovative. I also had banana bread and they were very generous and gave me 2 big slices. The service is very good and the staff nice.
The place itself is small and cozy, with power outlets and free wifi.
The barista is very nice and patient, and explained the different types of coffee.
I really liked coming, and will be coming again!!

I came in for the first time and the service was amazing. The barista helped me pick out the horchata latte with rum-infused beans, and it tasted great. He even walked my coffee and water over to my table. It’s a very chill place to hang out or work since they have Wi-Fi.

We felt very good here, and the sunset is truly beautiful. The staff were very friendly, and I appreciate how well they managed everything considering the large number of guests.

The DJ played really good music, but in my opinion the volume could have been a bit higher. I would have loved to have a real party here.

Great views at night, you can come without reservation and wait around 20 mins. Good drinks. For dinner, would strongly recommend to make a reservation inside. The terrace is filled with stand people.

The most unfriendly and inhospitable bar I've been to in my travels around the world. The waitress/host (didn't catch the name, earpiece in) barely acknowledged our existence as we arrived. We seated ourselves, she approached us with a menu  handed us it and said nothing else, just walked away. No welcomes, no recommendations, nothing. We both speak Spanish. Then to make matters even more ignorant the same waitress/host returned to our table, simply stood there, we said our drink orders, she took the menu turned and left. Again zero communication. When we figured out we had to pay at the bar, the same host took our payment and waited for receipt whilst talking to her colleagues. My wife said thank you. Unsurprisingly, the host did not respond. Terrible, cold, unfriendly and rude service. The drinks were bang average.

Wow, wow! Amazing coffee, first time we had this PR coffee! I will have this again 1000% it was delicious and so fresh. Given to me in a bag which is so cool. Customer service was A1. Tiny location but still was worth the stop.

Great views! Try to make reservation, or else it’s hard to get a table or even enter. We arrived 10min before they opened (6pm) without reservation. They told us we could stand in line outside and we would enter, but we were told we couldn’t seat in any area as all seated areas were for reservations. They started letting reservations enter first, and around 15min later they started allowing access to everyone else on the “no reservations” line. Once we entered we saw many available seats, but they told us we couldn’t seat until 6:45pm (which must have been their limit for any other person who had reservations). So we had to stand and order our drinks directly at the bar…. & Finally after 6:45pm everybody without table was able to look for a free table or seating area, and simply seat and stay there! (The process was a bit of a hassle with all the specific waiting times and so on… but well, I assume if you have a reservation it must be hassle free). All in all, we had a good time! We were able to find a great table, with an awesome view, and had a wonderful time! Drinks were good! And once we were seated we could order drinks to a waiter, and service was good. Drinks were also quite affordable. (You expect a Skybar like this to be overpriced, but it was a nice surprise to see average normal drinks prices here)

To keep it simple, this place is amazing. The food was amazing. The chicken and steak were more than scrumptious. The drinks were amazing. Views were amazing. Only drawback (if you could call it that) was the service. The servers weren’t as attentive as we expected them to be, but when they did visit our table, they were very nice. I personally would suggest getting dinner on the 19th or 20th floor then visiting the rooftop for a drink after (which is what we did). Overall this place is definitely worth the price you pay.

author_name: Off The Beaten Path Adventures and Travel (Adventures)
text:
I heard for years how Argentina has the best meat in the world. This place definitely lives up to that reputation.  It is so good that a local that knows all of the best spots recommend it.

Thing to know, is to make a reservation.  You will also want to have pants. We were in shorts and had to change.  It makes sense, it is a nicer place.

The view was amazing. It is on top of the building.  You can see all around the city. We had walked ten miles that day, and it was fun pointing out the places that we had gone to. You can also spot future places to explore.

Most importantly,  the food was amazing. We got an amazing steak. I had waited so long to try out the steak. I also had the sushi. It came with quite a few pieces. I really enjoyed the flavor.

The service was top notch too. Our waiter was way nice and attentive. He checked to see how we were. When we asked for things like bread and olive oil, he was quick to get it. I would highly recommend this place.  I would even say it is a must when you visit Buenas Aires.

Really nice view over the city of Buenos Aires from the rooftop bar. There are two terraces you can pick from, the second of which wasn’t crowded at all. The service wad excellent. The hotel overall is very impressive and the bar experience was as expected.

We had an absolutely incredible experience. The sunset views are awesome and the food (we had starters and selection of sushi) was fantastic. The bar has a super nice vibe (including lounge music) and atmosphere.

My husband & I made an advance reservation for a special evening with drinks and lite bites, while enjoying the sunset at this upscale rooftop bar. We had read the reviews & were looking forward to a classy, relaxing evening with wonderful views of BA. When we first arrived there was easy listening music being played on the sound system, but about 5 minutes later, the volume was turned up very high & a DJ started playing club/dancing type music that did not fit the atmosphere, the clientele or an upscale location. We could hardly hear each other talk & it was not a relaxing. I mentioned the music volume and genre choice to the manager, but nothing changed. We noticed several couples get up and leave only an hour into their 2 hour reservation windows and assumed they felt like we did. We both ordered a  total of 2 drinks each, but decided not to order food because we were not enjoying the loud, dance club music that was being played. We asked for our bill and also left early, before our 2 hour reservation window was over. For perspective, our ages are 57 & 65- still relatively young & we still know how to have fun dancing/partying, but this was not what we came to the rooftop bar for. What we would say to the management is to know your clientele, understand why this location is visited & have the environment reflect that.

The view is amazing! Try to make a reservation earlier and find a table closer to the edge.

They made me a mocktail margarita, sushi was okay. Rest of the food was nice.

Looks like a really nice hotel and the history behind the Alvear family is quite interesting.

Service was good and they have a minimum for how much you have to order so eat and drink up!

This is on top of one of the most luxurious hotels in Argentina: the Alvear Palace.  The hotel has an elegant, classic style,  with reminiscences of the glorious ’20s in Argentina (Belle Epoque).  The Bar is on the 11th floor, on an open-air terrace with great views of Recoleta.  It offers light dishes and signature drinks.  Address: Alvear Av. 1891.

This is one of those hidden gems—tucked inside Casa Lucia Boutique Hotel, without a doubt one of the nicest spots in town for top-tier cocktails and delicious bites. It’s the perfect place for a date, a quiet business meeting, or simply to escape the noise of the big city.

If you’re in Recoleta, I highly recommend visiting this place.
Amazing drink, we had the dry martini, white vermouth, and Nuevo rumbo. Great service by Ravel. Beautiful atmosphere, with background music, perfect vibes for a drink
Grand bar in the Lobby of the boutique hotel Casa Lucia (Former Sofitel). Soaring space with a glass panel ceiling

Great service from host Ana, server Francisco, bartender Luciana and Ravel. Amazing cocktails, bites and vibes. We were guests at the hotel for four nights and enjoyed the Club Bacan each evening to end our day. Please come support the amazing staff and excellence of Casa Lucía
Second time here.  Last time was 5 months ago.  Great latte with beautiful latte art.  Nice people.  Right around the corner from Standard East Village and Bowery hotels.

The owner was fantastic. The drinks were great. I have had Matcha in other coffee shops but this by far was the best MATCHA I have had. My family members also had coffee drinks and quesitos and it was great. Will come again.

The vibe and atmosphere is cozy and intimate. I ordered the horchata latte and it was perfectly “not too sweet”. This is also a fun place because of swings at the window where you can look out while enjoying a coffee. Daniella & Nikki were amazing!

Fantastic coffee, made by servers who clearly care about the store and brand, and the caliber of what they prepare for customers. It’s rare to see this kind of work ethic and conscientiousness, politeness and consideration, all combined in one store. Delicious quesito, fabulous tres leches flavor coffee. A must have if you’re on 14th street and you want exceptional coffee several levels above anything  else in the area. It’s a chain so look for them in any neighborhood: wishing them every continued success.

Love that the coffee beans come directly from Puerto Rico and this place is PR owned. Pretty cool and creative spot.  I had the coquito latte and the quesito. Both were so good! Also loved the ambiance and amazing service. Julissa is amazing. We will be back!

Absolutely fantastic. As someone from the u.s I had a blast here. I don't even drink, nor does my fiancee.

I came here the first time alone, looking for places to take my fiancee who is Argentinan, to go to after work.

I do not know español well. They were very patient with me and went above and beyond for me. Extremely friendly.

I had their hamburger. It was so good that I brought her here the next night.

We got pasta, provoleta, a hamburger and a chocolate dessert. Every dish was so very good we were amazed because themed restaurants usually have bad food but great decor. This was both.

The theme is amazing. Truly beautiful recreation of an old outdoor u.s alley. The dining room is beautiful.

They put so much into the theme, I could really go ob but I recommend you go yourself.

I've eaten at a lot of restaurants in ba, this was my favorite.

Music is way too loud though. But they said they'd turn it down on request. I really can't complain.

This restaurant offers an incredible experience! 🔥 The atmosphere is fantastic, the service is excellent, and the food is quite good. I’d definitely return for the drinks and appetizers. The sushi was decent—not extraordinary—but the overall experience is what really stands out. That said, the vibe sets high expectations, so you might wish the food matched it. Go in with an open mind, and you’ll likely be pleasantly surprised! 🫰🏾

The immersive experience is amazing and a lot of fun! However, beware that they have recently changed their policies. Before, the drink tickets you bought at the door could be used for most of the drinks on the menu - a well-curated and interesting set of drinks! But now your drink tickets can only be used for a much smaller selection of about 6 options. It was very disappointing to learn - after we had already paid at the door for two drinks each - that if I wanted my favorite “Machine Gun Kelly” I would have to purchase it completely separately and at full price. It’s definitely worth a trip just for the experience but the value to us as repeat customers has gone down with this change.

super nice theme and look, though the cocktails are quite pricey and not really delicious. my friend could not finish any of her two cocktails (and that doesn’t happen often). still, nice staff and great views

The place is nice for pictures, but food is mediocre nuggets from supermarket are better that the Thai chicken that they have, not Thai at all, then drinks were bad , the worse gin tonic ever no bubbles no lemon on the drink. Too expensive for the common food that serve. They waiters were nice though.

Exquisite spirit-forward cocktails prepared with care in a plush bar. Bartenders were friendly and clear experts at their craft. The Jasmin Blanco cocktail was especially good, a mix of gin with jasmine tea and honey, but the classic martinis were also a hit with our group. Great breadth of selection for differing palettes.

I went to Verne Club and had a really good time. It’s a stylish spot with a cool atmosphere, perfect for a night out in Palermo. The cocktails were genuinely good — well-made, balanced, and with interesting flavors. The service was friendly and the staff seemed to really know what they were doing behind the bar. It can get a bit busy, but the vibe is still comfortable and enjoyable. Overall, Verne Club is a great place if you’re looking for quality drinks and a nice night out. I’d definitely go back.

Great vibes, very dimly lit and ambient! The drinks were okay — I ordered a tiramisu martini off the menu, which wasn’t my favorite (and didn’t really get tipsy) but maybe their menu items are a bit more promising! There’s a lot of chairs so it’s a bit crowded in there and therefore it gets loud when there’s a lot of people, but I give bonus points for a sectioned off smoking area/courtyard area

Rating: 3.2/5 [dine in]

Saw great reviews for this spot, so we decided to swing by. Service was great, and they spoke English as well, which was helpful. Unfortunately, the drinks didn’t quite live up to the hype compared to other cocktail bars we tried that day.

White Jasmine ($11,500 ARS): Heraclito London Dry Gin, Cardamom, Lemon Honey, Jasmine Tea (ABV 18.8%). Better than the 1869. You can pick up the flavors, but the lemon honey aftertaste was a bit too strong.

1869 ($12,500 ARS): Havana 7 Rum, Zuelo Torrontés Vinegar, Dates, Pomegranate (ABV 19.6%). Sweet, but the alcohol flavor overpowered everything else. Also, the foam topping was too heavy.

decent drinks but shockingly bad service for a bar that aspires to be Top50. 10 waiters in a place with 4 customers, all occupied with anything else but to serve the guests. Left after 20min with an empty glass.

It’s a steal! 😎 Cocktail omakase is usually expensive, so this price is honestly amazing. It also comes with small delicious dishes. You can choose from non-alcoholic, low ABV, or spirited options. Highly recommended!

Loved this cocktail omakase experience. The drinks and small bites were all thoughtfully paired and worked really well together. I chose the low-alcohol menu, and I really liked that they offer three different menu options! Even a non-alcoholic option, which is such a nice touch. Honestly, for the price, this experience felt like an amazing value. The staff were all incredibly kind and welcoming too. Such a great atmosphere overall, definitely coming back next time!

Amazing experience!! So unique and felt like being back in Tokyo. The staff is super accommodating (we needed to switch last minute to the vegetarian option and that was super easy). The drinks are super innovative and diverse so you’ll feel like you’re getting a really unique experience. It was the perfect amount of alcohol for a 1 hour seating across 4 drinks for the Spirited menu (1st one was nonalcoholic). The bites paired beautifully! For the current price, this really is worth it! I really think everyone should give this place a try!!! can’t wait to come back and try another menu!
Love the vibe and most importantly the amazing drinks! Come for pre-dinner drinks or night cap. The bartenders are cute and knowledgeable and friendly and chatty.

Really great cocktail experience. While it's five drinks, by alcohol amount it works out to a much more reasonable alcohol equivalent of about 2.5 drinks.

The cocktails are excellently crafted and the small bites pair well with them.

Looking forward to returning!

A Memorable Birthday Night at Crystal Bar!

I discovered Crystal Bar while looking for restaurants in Buenos Aires to celebrate my birthday on November 19 during my vacation in Argentina. I chose Crystal Bar because of the beautiful views of Puerto Madero. The information online said the restaurant opens at 6:00 p.m. and that reservations are not required. Since Argentinians usually eat dinner late, we thought arriving at 7:00 p.m. would allow us to get a nice table with a view.

That did not happen. All of the tables with views were already taken, so we were seated at the eastern end of the restaurant, overlooking the residential side of Puerto Madero. The restaurant was very elegant and the service was excellent, so we were not too disappointed about missing the ideal view.

I should have researched the menu more carefully because there were limited vegetarian options available for dinner. The cocktails were outstanding and the atmosphere, including the electronic chill music, was perfect.

One challenge is the layout of the tables. It is difficult to take photos of the Puerto Madero views without disturbing other guests unless you are seated at one of the view tables.
Although my birthday dinner turned out differently than I expected, the ambiance, service, great cocktails and reasonable prices made the experience worthwhile. Next time, I will arrive when the restaurant opens and I will study the menu more closely.

Thank you for a memorable birthday.

Gorgeous panoramic view of Puerto Madero and the city.

The menu complements the view. It includes a descent variety of sushi, appetizers and sandwiches. The food maintains the high caliber of the Alvear Hotel gastronomy. It will not disappoint.

The service is impeccable. Warm and friendly staff.

Ridiculous price to sit and enjoy the sunset! 9000 pesos per person minimum to sit on chairs that are in bad shape. The food was average and the drinks, meh! The 3 stars are for the view and that’s it.
See all these pictures in the reviews and save your money. Sorry Crystal Bar, we were not impressed 🤷🏻‍♀️

We were visiting from Los Angeles and walked all the way from San Telmo Market to catch the sunset, only to be turned away for wearing shorts, in the middle of summer, with no clear dress code posted.

Disappointing experience, wish they were more friendly and welcoming to visitors. Posting this so others are aware.

The bar is at the rooftop of a fancy hotel, but don’t worry we were not on our best suit but we were treated as any other customer. The drinks are expensive but the view of Puerto Madero is one of a kind. I would recommend to have a drink at sunset to enjoy the view

One of the best pourovers I've had in a cafe in a long time! Loved this cafe. The baristas are super nice and very skilled, which is hard to find in a lot of the country.

I ordered a washed anaerobic Colombian from their Competition series. Sometimes these coffees can be a bit over fermented for my taste, but this was probably the best anaerobic I've ever tried. The barista brewed in an origami dripper. Such a nice acidity and balanced flavor that was sweet and fruity tasting. My wife got a hot chocolate that she very much enjoyed as well.

Coffee Project New York is hands down one of the best coffee spots I’ve ever visited in the city! The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, with the aroma of freshly brewed coffee filling the air. Their baristas are incredibly skilled and friendly — they truly care about every cup they serve. I tried their signature deconstructed latte, and it was an experience in itself — rich, smooth, and beautifully balanced. The minimalist decor and chill vibe make it perfect for relaxing or getting some work done. Definitely a must-visit for true coffee lovers in NYC!

Coffee Project in the East Village is a small, modern spot with a Euro-café vibe (bonus points for the no-laptop policy). Their medium-roast blend—Colombia meets Guatemala—hits a sweet spot: smooth milk-chocolate body with a caramel finish, zero bitterness, and plenty of flavor.

I ordered an almond croissant, and the staff offered to pop it in the oven. It came out crisp outside, soft and fragrant inside—no sogginess. They even let us enjoy a couple of pastries we’d picked up elsewhere at their outdoor table, all with genuine warmth.

Friendly people, thoughtful touches, and a medium roast I’d gladly order again. Perfect coffee-and-pastry stop in the neighborhood.

Phenominal coffee selection here. A decent breadth of flavors. I had an anaerobic Columbian coffee that was the perfect balance of jammy, funky ferment while being light bodied and quite sweet.

The barista was nice and helped me pick a coffee. I was inclined towards a Myanmar coffee simply because I've never tried one before, but she steered me to the Columbian one and that was a great choice.
Good experience at Libros del Pasaje.

We came here for lunch and had a great experience overall everything was spot on, from the food to the atmosphere.

A special thanks to Asul, who truly stood out. As people from the industry ourselves, we really appreciated her high level of professionalism, deep knowledge of wines, and overall experience. She guided us perfectly and made the whole lunch feel effortless and enjoyable.

Definitely a place we’d happily return to
Excellent service, great vibe and exquisite venue. Alexis took great care of me. The cigar humidor and selection was extensive. Highly recommend!!

Gorgeous rooftop bar close to Plaza de Mayo overlooking the river. Service from the door all the way through lunch was fantastic! Our server, Azul, was the best! Spoke great English and was so sweet! Our drinks were all delish. Highly recommend the mojito :) We also got the charcuterie, which was perfect for sharing between the four of us. Highly recommend this special spot!

Beautiful terrace with a decent view. Very pleasant to sit out and enjoy the day. Also a great inside cigar lounge. Service was friendly and correct, but slow and more or less absent. Long stretches of time with no one on staff present on the terrace. And while there were no more than half a dozen people having lunch, it took 50 minutes to get a milanesa - which is also probably why it was overcooked. The fantastic potatoes and spicy chimichurri almost made up for that.

Had an amazing experience at Piso15 Skybar. The views over the city are genuinely spectacular — one of those spots where Buenos Aires feels endless and alive.

I ordered the milanesa de ojo de bife con hueso and it was outstanding: perfectly cooked, super tender, and full of flavor. Portion size was generous and beautifully served, definitely a standout dish.

Service was friendly and attentive without being intrusive. Great cocktails, great ambiance, and overall a place I’d come back to without hesitation — whether for a casual dinner or a night out with a view.

Highly recommended.

Found this little shop on a whim and it did not disappoint! The vibe is instantly set with tons of fresh indoor plants and Puerto Rican flags (Hello? Bad Bunny? Wya?)
We ordered a Conquito & Horchata latte both with oat milk. 10/10.
Cool thing about this place is that they have 3 different bean choices as well that they roast themselves.
Cindy was so helpful with suggestions and handled multiple customers seamlessly and with care 🤍🖤

Would def return when I come back to NYC.

Awesome coffee find!
Double espresso was perfect! You can choose the variety of bean.
Also tried a cream cheese and honey pastry this was also great.
Great vibes, close to Penn Station, and funky interior.
Server/owner was very friendly.
Great experience, put this one on your list of most visits if you want seriously good coffee.

Best coffee option near penn station, opens early. Plus lots of outlets. They were out of tres leches so I got dulce de leche instead. It was good—but the horchata was DIVINE! Shout out Cindy for beautiful latte art & kind energy ☺️

FINALLY a good coffee!
Brenda and her team are super friendly and create such a welcoming atmosphere.
We’d been searching for a good coffee for five days, and after visiting this place, we can finally say — we found it!
Delicious coffee, cozy vibes, and the perfect spot to take a break from the busy city.
If you’re looking for a great coffee within walking distance of Madison Square Garden, this is the place to go! ☕💛

Stopped in here spontaneously and am so happy that I did. When you choose a latte, they have you choose which beans you want. I had the RumChata, which was so excellent!! I also grabbed a fresh quesito for a snack. I’ll be back!

I had a wonderful experience at 787 Coffee in the West Village. The coffee was excellent — rich, smooth, and clearly high quality — but what truly made the visit special was the service.

Ammie was incredibly kind, attentive, and welcoming. She took the time to explain the menu, made great recommendations, and genuinely made us feel comfortable and well taken care of. Her positive energy and professionalism really stood out and elevated the whole experience.

The atmosphere is cozy and authentic, exactly what you want from a neighborhood coffee shop in NYC. I’ll definitely be coming back — not just for the coffee, but for the amazing service as well. Highly recommend!

Amazing coffee! Do not miss. They make their own blends with unique flavours; coffee is smooth with no bitter after taste. Try the coquito and the horchata flavoured lattes with rum beans. Thank you for a lovely afternoon coffee!

I love how quiet and cozy it is, makes it a perfect place to do some work or chill. The service here is super kind and sweet as well. I got the cinnamon rum-infused coffee and it’s sooo good, so fragrant. Will be coming here more!!

the smell of coffee hits like a warm blanket when you walk in. they were out of the whiskey beans but madeup for it by being precious.  Rachel was wonderful- what a great coffee experience. and i just found out that its named 787 for its origin story: the area code for Puerto Rico (also in America) by Martin, who was ‘here’ but ‘not here’ (i saw nothing…).

also, great merch for sale.

787 Coffee is such a cozy and welcoming space with an amazing vibe. The atmosphere is warm, inviting, and perfect for relaxing, working, or catching up with friends. The coffee is absolutely delicious — rich, smooth, and clearly made with care and quality beans.

What makes this place even more special is the history behind it. You can truly feel the passion and story in every cup, which adds a deeper meaning to the experience. From the friendly staff to the thoughtfully designed space, everything comes together beautifully.

If you’re looking for great coffee, a cozy environment, and a place with heart and history, 787 Coffee is a must-visit ☕️✨
Too crowded.
Good experience at Oporto Almacén.
Too crowded.

From the moment I stepped into the café, it felt like being wrapped in a warm hug. The space was cozy and inviting, filled with a gentle buzz of conversation and the comforting aroma of fresh coffee. What truly made the experience special, though, was the kindness behind the counter. Martin, the barista, greeted me with such genuine warmth that it instantly set the tone for my visit.

When I asked for a recommendation, Martin suggested the whiskey dulce de leche coffee—and wow, it was unforgettable. Smooth, rich, and perfectly balanced, it had just the right hint of sweetness from the dulce de leche and a subtle depth from the whiskey that made every sip feel indulgent without being overwhelming. It was clear that this wasn’t just a random suggestion, but a drink he truly believed in.

This café isn’t just about great coffee; it’s about the people and the feeling you leave with. Thanks to Martin’s excellent recommendation and the café’s sweet, welcoming atmosphere, it’s a place I’ll be excited to return to again and again.

Amazing place!
The food and service were excellent - everything was delicious and beautifully presented. The atmosphere is very cozy and welcoming, and the music is at a perfect volume, so you can actually enjoy a conversation without shouting (which is rare and appreciated!).

And the churros… absolutely stole my heart 🤍 Crispy, warm, and just perfect.

Definitely a place I’ll be coming back to. Highly recommend!

Christmas vibes and great creative cocktails. Loved this place and it’s def on my return list when I come back. Such a fun and festive bar. The cocktail with coffee liquor is so goood wow and plus it’s strong 🎄🎅

I really want to try the espresso martini stuff next time looks fun!

Went for a bite and drinks after visiting intrepid. It was nice. Enough sitting area as it was 3 floors. I wish portion size of fries were a bit large. Loved the pork belly bao.
My complain is they didn’t give salt in the rim of margarita. Otherwise nice place.

This is, quite possibly, the cutest bar I’ve ever been to. It was all decorated for Christmas, and everything felt so happy and joyful and cozy! I had an excellent espresso martini, and my husband had an amazing drink called Famously Naked. Both were delish! Service was fairly prompt and very friendly! Loved it here!!!

Didn’t have any food, just came in to the bar for drinks but Jen was amazing. The drinks were amazing. The white chocolate espresso martini was to die for. Highly recommend and will definitely be back! Thanks Jen for the great drinks and great talks!

787 Coffee in Manhattan was really nice. I stopped by while walking across Manhattan during an exploratory vacation. Out of all the options, I landed on the mint cold brew pouch, which was a little pricey but 1) it was huge and 2) it’s New York City.

The mint cold brew was super tasty! It had a deep uniform flavor with a clean mint finish. It was also convenient for carrying around the city. The cafe itself is small, but it’s comfortable, cozy, and has a homey, welcoming vibe. Overall I had a nice experience.

My mom and I love coming to the 787 location on 15th street. Today we came in and Matthew was so welcoming and accommodating. We came during the blizzard and were very happy with our experience. Would highly recommend!! The coffee is also incredible.

Ordered a coquito latte and had a lovely conversation with the Barista. I love small local shops that allows you to get an intimate experience. Had the pleasure of speaking about my family coming from Ciales, PR and supported local small coffee company and bought Nuevayol coffee beans. They were recommended by the Barista.

My go-to for coffee around my job. Great taste and quality coffee with rum, whiskey, or supremo infused beans ( no it’s not actually alcohol so you won’t get drunk) but tastes amazing. Coquito and Dulce de leche are my go-tos. Nice chill vibe too,

This is a cute, cozy place on 15 st off 8th ave. Nice space to settle in with a book or get some work done. The wifi works well. The barista Elly is super nice and gave us impeccable service, even tweaked our coffee to our taste. They do the 787 flavors pretty well. Enjoyed my time there and definitely wants to continue supporting this place .

787 Coffee was an absolute standout experience in NYC — incredible coffee, warm atmosphere, and a true passion for craft you can taste in every sip. The espresso was smooth and rich, and you can tell they care deeply about sourcing and roasting well.

Chris, our barista, completely elevated the visit — he took the time to walk us through the beans, helped us choose the perfect option, and made us feel like regulars even though it was our first time in. His energy, knowledge, and genuine hospitality made this an easy 11/10 stop. We’ll absolutely be back.

Awesome little spot!!  I was here on vacation from North Carolina and this puerto rican cafe was exactly what I needed to make me feel right at home I loved it.  The whiskey infused coffee was absolutely delicious and the quesitos were amazing suggested by Chris who was great.  So happy we found this spot.

A fantastic local coffee shop that I stumbled upon near my hotel. Moises was warm and accommodating, providing details on each of the coffees and sharing other things to do in the local area. Highly recommend the coquito latte and homemade banana bread! I will definitely be back the next time I’m in town. :)

I was walking by this rather new coffee spot recently and decided to stop and get a coffee.  Ordered an Americano and it was delicious.  They advised that the owners have a coffee farm in Puerto Rico and all the beans are sourced from there.  It is a tine place with a couple of seats inside, another space upstairs and a couple of tables outside.
I very nice experience.

The coffee was perfect. Not too sweet, so much flavor, and one of the smoothest lattes I’ve had. All nestled in a cozy space with comfy seating. And big thanks to Deborah for providing such great and friendly service!

My first time trying 787 Coffee and I am quite amazed. I got a whiskey infused Mazapan latte and it tasted just like the Mazapan I’ve had growing up. My friends recommended this spot to me and it certainly lived up to its hype. I would 100% recommend this place to everyone. The service was also very lovely, I felt very welcomed and was recommended on what to get.

It’s also not too sweet and I’d say it was perfectly balanced

It’s been 24 years since I’ve visited NY. It being a last minute thing, it seemed everywhere I went didn’t have a sufficient cup of coffee. I didn’t even attempt to get it out of knowing it was going to be garbage.

We drove past this location heading to Canal street. I instant knows I had to try it especially being Puerto Rican coffee runs in my veins.

Before heading back home I told myself I had to stop here and may I tell you I was NOT DISAPPOINTED AT ALL..

The coquito coffee was phenomenal and you could literally taste everything from. The coffee to the coquito to the cinnamon and chacho mang, I could have drank 2 more cups on the jump of how good it was 🤤😋

But of course I didn’t just try the coffee. Milo had just brought out a fresh batch of cookies. Like literally out the oven. I bought 3 and while driving back to Buffalo and fighting over the last bite, I instantly regretting not cleaning out the remainder cookies.

This was a dope find and 100% recommend this place!!!!!!!

I came over the summer and I’m never usually in this area — but today I was and I knew I had to come back. Perfect place to come with your laptop, drink fantastic coffee and eat the freshly baked cookies (I ordered my second cup and couldn’t resist, as the cookies were filling the cafe up with the best smell ever). I spent all day working from a little corner. While other cafes may make a face at you for spending your time there, 787 does not. One of the workers even poured me a cup of water in the middle of the day which was just so comforting.

This location is so friendly and helpful, I feel like whenever I ask baristas in other cafe’s what they recommend, they just say whatever first comes to mind. At 787, I asked for their recommendations and they really took a moment to respond and give me their best rec.

I tried the Tres Leches latte and used the Whiskey blend at the recommendation of one of the baristas, Becca and I’m so glad I did. I also got a chicken empanada for breakfast and it was great.

Later on I had a cortado with the Rum blend, at the recommendation of Milo and it was so comforting when I needed an additional mid-day pick me up. That cortado with a big freshly baked cookie was chefs kiss :)

While walking by we just decided to stop by and WOW, very happy we did! The coquito latte and Dulce de leche latte made by Makayla was so good! Most of the coffees we’ve had in NY were slightly bitter but not this one, the espresso shot was done to perfection! Milo that took our order was very nice and explained to us the coffee beans that have available, definitely would recommend!

Honestly wasn’t expecting to find such a cute little cafe in such a busy part of the Upper East Side/Midtown area….my wife and I stopped by while we were heading to a hospital checkup…place is tiny, but that’s actually what made it special for us 🥰🥰 When we walked in, there were only two people there — a guy named Singh and a lady probably working with him. Because the place is so small and cozy, we ended up having a really nice conversation with Singh and quickly became friends. We even took a photo together an I just uploaded. Before we left, he told us that next time our coffee would be on the house. We also agreed to meet again the next time we come for a hospital checkup … March 25th. We’ll probably be there around 1 PM, or maybe 2 PM at the latest. If you read this hello Singh 😉😉 Just wanted to leave this here for anyone who comes across this place. I really loved the atmosphere and the people. Hopefully we’ll be back for another latte on March 25th before our appointment. Maybe we’ll see some of you there too! ☕️

I’ve been going to this location for a while now. My girlfriend orders the same thing every time because it’s just so good: a hot Coquito latte with rum beans and oat milk. She says it’s really cozy to drink and reminds her of family traditions (she’s Puerto Rican btw). She had to rep a Puerto Rican Owned business! Honestly, it’s really good from what I’ve tasted. My girlfriend thinks so too so we always come back!! Dress is a super cool barista, super nice and easy to talk to. You get in a convo and the next second your drink is done. We thought it deserved a shoutout for coming here so often! Service- amazing!! The drinks absolutely amazing!! Atmosphere- super calm and cozy! Love going here!!

Compact cafe serving Puerto Rican flavor inspired coffee drinks. The high-end beans make for an expensive yet flavorful cup. I tried a quesito, a small cheese-filled layered pastry with honey glaze, and it was a lovely combo with coquito latte. But with tax and tip I hit $20. The space is small with four seats. So you likely will be taking anything to go.

Leslie welcomed me and helped me pick out a delicious treat. Fun & warm  environment. I had the coquito latte with the rum beans. I also got a slice of the gluten free lemon poppyseed loaf. Definitely will be back.

The horchata latte was really smooth and didn’t have an overpowering cinnamon flavor.  I got it with oat milk which paired well with the horchata flavor. It had the right amount of sweetness. The barista was also very nice and inviting, and actually recommended the horchata latte! Would definitely come back here again.

Let’s make one thing clear: you’re here for the beautiful sit-in-a-greenhouse vibe. Cause it’s truly beautiful and calming.
I’d recommend wearing sunglasses!!! It’s sunny.
HOWEVER it took 15 minutes to get up the elevator before our meal, and no one at the hotel could let us up. The elevator was just not functioning. It was insane.
The service was also slow and slightly forgetful.
The food is good, no complaints. Just not memorable either. Classic brunch.
They have gluten free bread.
The coffee is sub-par.
Cocktails are good!!

Such an amazing place! Love the vibe, delicious food and beautifully creative cocktails!! Our server Kamila was wonderful, so friendly and gave great recommendations! Highly recommend going to The Woo Hoo NYC for a great experience!
Good experience at Café Cuervo.
Too crowded.

My mom and I came for dinner following a birthday spa day. The food was amazing! Packed with flavor and the drinks were delicious. The service was great. Our server, Eva, was so sweet and patient and went above and beyond to ensure I had a great birthday dinner. I truly felt taken care of by her and her team. Lastly, the atmosphere was breathtaking. Being on the roof was an obvious perk but the ambiance was magnificent. My only recommendation would be to add heaters in every dining space of the establishment. Other than that, great experience!

Very nice and cozy place with a great atmosphere. The food and cocktails were delicious. Daniella was absolutely lovely and friendly, which made the experience even better. We never felt rushed and were able to fully enjoy our time. Highly recommend!

What a pleasure spending my 27th birthday at Vintage Green. We were hosted by Stephen who greeted us with an enamoring smile and a warm presence taking us to a cozy table overlooking the city skyline. Our 8 top was immediately greeted by Eva who shared her insider scoop on the best menu items. Our drinks were crisp and delish, the food perfectly sized for sharing. Our group couldn’t stop giggling and smiling and we can’t wait to come back again! Definitely a 10/10 place to spend a birthday, special occasion, or night out with your girls! #honeymarmalade

Gorgeous ambiance of a rooftop bar— even in winter. Felt whimsical, cozy, and elegant all at once. Stephen the host felt like an instant best friend who I would’ve loved to have dining at my table! But alas, as he was working, instead I was accompanied by a delicious, large portion of seasonal fresh pasta and a glass of wine. No complaints.

The Dead Rabbit has a great name and an even more interesting setting.....once inside.  The deco of the place speaks to the history of the area.  The lobster devil eggs were unbelievable.  I ordered the house drink "Doctors Orders" and it was amazing....I had 3. Melissa was our server and she was great.

I highly recommend this place and its a must visit!!

Is it one of the best - yes.

Big hype about the Guinness, and it really didn’t disappoint. Love the sawdust and the total vibe.

Staff were great - and we have a brilliant time, make sure you take a dollar bill so you can write a little note and the team will staple it to the wall for you.

They sad they never ever take these down, so it’s a nice thought to return some day and see the note you might have written years ago.

ENJOY!

Definitely an iconic place and super busy no matter when you go. The food was good. Our party ordered multiple different drinks and all were good--Dr's Orders is the one to try. It was made clear we had 2 hours to sit, eat and drink because reservations are stacked, so not a place to lounge and take your time with friends. Was fun to try.

The Dead Rabbit is hands down my favorite bar in NYC. The bartenders are incredible—knowledgeable, friendly, and always professional, even when the place is buzzing. And trust me, it does get busy, so be prepared for a lively crowd.

I usually hang out in the Tap Room, which has the perfect pub energy, but the Parlor upstairs is an absolute must-visit. Definitely make a reservation for the Parlor—it’s worth it.

The Dead Rabbit is all about great Irish pub food, top-tier cocktails, and one of the best pints of Guinness you’ll find anywhere in NYC. A classic for a reason. 🍀🍺

We had a reservation for parlor for my 10k birthday. The Irish coffee was the best! They also so kindly gave us tiramisu for free and switched the drinks we found too strong with some gentler ones. Loved it!

Beautiful atmosphere, great service. I was not sure what to expect from the food because of so many mixed reviews, here is my honest breakdown:
1. Whitefish Caesar salad 10/10. A must try, I can eat a pound of this it was so delicious.
2. Short rib pasta is slept on. It is one of the best pasta dishes with thr softest short rib I’ve ever tired
3. The burger is very good. Better than Au Cheval, but not mind blowing. It is just very good, but not a “must” get
4. Crab Wontons were a fun app, the sauce is delicious, I would get these again
5. Carrot cake was incredible. 10/10

Loved it!! Had no reservation, so showed up just before opening time and got a great table in bar area. Really friendly staff and wonderful food. The monkey bread still has me dreaming about it!! Great cocktails too. Atmosphere was fantastic and we were not rushed out. Will definitely be back next time we’re in NYC!

We had an excellent experience at monkey bar. I’ve been wanting to go for years and finally got my shot to go on my girlfriend’s birthday.

When you walk in, it’s not clear where to go in the front area. There should be someone for first timers making sure you know to walk in towards the hostess, which is not visible from the front when it’s busy (which is all the time). I stood there like an idiot for 30 seconds. We waited 15 minutes for our reservation that we showed up on time for. No one told us we could order a cocktail as we watched others (smarter than us) do that.

Chris was one of the best waiters I’ve ever had in New York. The ambience is spectacular. The reason for docking a star is because when you have a reputation and pricing like monkey bar, I expect perfection and the food was great but not perfect. The monkey bread was insane, best thing on the menu. I don’t think bread can get much better than that. The Caesar salad was a little disappointing. I had to remind my girlfriend there was potato chips in it because they had gotten soggy sitting and dressing. Chu Toro tuna was great but the crisp bread it came with it was underwhelming. Crab Rangoon’s were good but not great. Important note is that Chris threw in the monkey bread and crab Rangoon’s and dessert on the house, which is so generous! Shoutout Chris.

Truffle mac & cheese was one of my favorite things. The crispy top is awesome. The steak frites was good. Steak was great, but the Bernaise sauce and fries were very average. Fries particularly confusing. The most disappointing thing on the menu was the famous French dip. I feel like a jerk saying this, but it tasted like something I would get at the bodega. I would enjoy it from the bodega, but that’s not where I’m at. Chocolate pie was fantastic. Would definitely come back, but the food was a bit below what I expected for such an impossible reservation. 4.3/5

Kamila, our sever was awesome! She brought out our food and drinks promptly and she was so helpful. I had the Sex Cures Anxiety drunk and the nacho fries. Both were awesome! I loved the place and all of the decor too.

The experience was so cute and fun!!! I loved entering the hallway with the awesome street art. The food was delicious! Not too much not to little. Drink were amazing but Camilla our waitress was the best! She was attentive and friendly! Loved this place and can’t wait to come back !

Worth the hype, ambiance is 10/10 we got seated a corner booth nice a private and roomy. Excellently crafted menu and the food was great our server was absolutely amazing. Honestly the best service I’ve ever had in a restaurant. The wagyu burger was melt in your mouth and the dry aged strip had the perfect crust. The mashed potatoes are a must, Some of the best I’ve ever had I can live without the creamed spinach and rangoons next time. But the truffle bread is definitely worth it, fluffy sweet savory and earthy. Truffle Mac is also excellent. Definitely going back to try even more of the menu. Honorable mention is the carrot cake. Super moist and spiced and very decently sized to share.

A gorgeous, old-school Upper East Side New York classic.

Service: Solid but unremarkable. The waiter was a bit hard to hear, though service overall was fine.

Food:
The menu is excellent with something for everyone, from lighter options to indulgent classics.
•	Crab cakes: Outstanding! crisp, golden exterior with a tender, incredibly meaty center.
•	White fish Caesar salad: The name gave me pause, but it worked beautifully. Light, fresh, and well-balanced. Enough to share for two, maybe three.
•	Burger: Heavenly. Juicy, perfectly cooked, and simply done.
•	French fries: The only letdown; lacked the crispness and salt.

Martinis: Top-notch—cold, clean, and expertly made.

Brunch at Monkey Thief did not come to play. The space is small and cozy, but the design is beautiful and gives off Southeast Asian vibes that really set the tone. The food was packed with flavor and felt like a fun twist on brunch favorites.

Everything we ordered hit the spot, especially the creativity behind each dish. That said, the lamb burger could’ve been better for the price. At $21, the patty was a lot smaller than expected, and there was more bun than burger. It looked great but just didn’t deliver on portion. Also, one of our dishes didn’t make it to the kitchen due to a mix-up, but we were fine skipping it and still left full and satisfied.

It’s the kind of spot that feels like a hidden gem. I’d definitely come back next time I’m visiting.

This trendy spot offers a brief menu from raw bar to fried chicken.  In line with their positioning as a cocktail bar, it is a rather loud space.  So I wouldn’t recommend for a quiet meal.  However, if you don’t mind the buzz the food is absolutely delicious.  Given the menu is largely small plates, we had an assortment of dishes and enjoyed every single one of them.

The Beef Koi was very good.  Diced and in a spicy sauce, it was a unique plating that packed a good spice.  The mash up of flavors in the croquettes was impressive.  Delicious cheesiness while also a mix of flavors from the chutneys.  The calamari was a big hit.  Not your typical calamari at all, this was proper pieces that had been lightly breaded and dashed with seasoning.  The sauce it came with added a kick.  Definitely worth trying.

We also had the Chicken Satay, Dumplings and Chicken Bao.  The chicken skewer was tender with a pretty standard peanut sauce, the dumplings packed a lot of flavor in their filling and the Chicken Bao had such good chicken on it, we ended up adding the Fried Chicken to our order.  And that was perhaps the best plate of all.  This bowl of boneless fried chicken, we ordered the large, was filled with taste.  The chicken was juicy and tender and the breading had a little spice to it with cumin and a mix of others.  This is the dish not to miss served along side of a cucumber salad.

From a drink perspective, we tried an assortment and you really can’t go wrong.  An upside for a crafted cocktail space, they were quite swift.  We had two rounds and at no point did it seem like we were waiting long for drinks.  The wait staff was also very quick to clear empty plates and bring water which was helpful given we ordered so much.

Helpful reviews? Check us out on IG @jjluxtravel

Stopped in on a busy night hoping they could squeeze us in for a table and fortunately they could! The atmosphere was very chill and there was vibrant conversation at the tables, but not so loud we couldn’t chat amongst ourselves. The tables were small, parties larger than 2 or 4 may struggle walking in and getting a table easily.

Great drink menu with a good variety of unique hand crafted cocktails. Food was delicious and there were quite a few dish options. The waitstaff gave us great advice on what to order and how to choose between cocktails and dishes. We came back for brunch on a different day and the menu was a bit more limited, but still delicious. My only negative feedback was we felt a bit “nickeled and dimed” when we needed additional sauce for a dish when we ran out - they charged us for the additional (which was less than a tablespoon portion size). We had spent plenty on other food and multiple cocktails, so it would have been nice if they “threw it in” without the extra charge. We plan to come back and bring friends the next time we’re in NYC.

Amazing food and cocktails, in this beautiful place! I loved the sesame inspired 7 Long Years cocktail, and the food was all amazing! The fries packed a flavorful punch, the croquettes were delicious, and the fried rice was perfectly crispy.
