Will definitely revisit to try out more from the menu, and would highly recommend the place!

Don’t get the bok choi—it’s too charred and it’s like a mouth of ashes. Ask for the bao to be warm—it’s not as good when the bao is kinda chilly… the temp and size makes it feel more like a happy hour appetizer. Drinks are fun, but on the sweet side and there’s not too many spirit forward options. Coconut salad nice but small. The rice was good, but wouldn’t have been notable without the egg. Server was super nice with a great smile.

Such a fun spot! The experience starts before you even walk in, you ring a bell and need a password to enter, which instantly sets the tone. Inside, the vibrant 80’s theme and playful decor create a lively, cheeky atmosphere that makes the place feel really unique. I ordered the Spice Box and it was delicious, crispy, flavorful, and perfect with drinks. Their cocktail menu is super creative too. I tried the “Shot Through the Heart” and it was smooth, well balanced, and memorable.

Definitely a place worth checking out if you want a quirky, energetic night out in the city.

Had an absolute blast celebrating our 17th anniversary here tonight!! The vibe is so cool, loved the music and the movies playing!! Drinks were really good and fun. Loved the Photo Booth and needing a password to get in is really fun. All the staff were so nice! Can’t recommend this place enough.
Good experience at Melbourne Café.
Too crowded.

Great and creative cocktails. The bartenders were super nice. The green elixir was far better than the strawberry gin one, which tasted artificial.

Beware the portion size for the steak tartare was definitely an appetizer size, even though it was listed as a “main” (with a very high price to match). Tasted good but wouldn’t order again only because it’s such a poor value.

Best New York City bar experience I’ve had! Joo was our bartender, and she was so knowledgeable about the drinks she made. I ordered something a little out of my norm, and she made sure I liked it because she could sense my hesitation. All the drinks we had were amazing! Joo made the entire experience worthwhile and we would come back again because of her, and of course the amazing drinks she makes! It was my birthday also and she made us feel special during our visit. I love the gin drink with dill and a gimlet shot she made for us! Thank you Joo!

Went here for a girls night and had a blast!! Special shout out to our server Anastasia she was a complete gem. The food was excellent from the apps to the main. Highly recommend the lobster noodles. The drinks were fabulous. The music wasn’t too loud and the vibes overall were great. I will be returning! 10/10

Giovanni gave us an amazing tasting experience leading to new creative cocktails that were fruity at times and herbal otherwise. And the sliders and seasonal apple burrata salad and poached pear desert were excellent! A fun after work or date atmosphere-we'll be back to sample more on the menu!

I heard this place has the world's second most diverse offering of gin varieties (the first being someplace in faraway Singapore). The gins are wonderful, the cocktails fantastic, the atmosphere delightful, and the service impeccable. Our bartender made excellent recommendations and worked with us to adjust cocktails so they had the perfect balance of dryness / smokeyness / spice for our taste. We felt super taken care of and left excited for our next visit (hopefully someday soon) so we might experience more of the exotic gin varieties offered here.

Second time visiting Tara Rose on a Friday night. It wasn’t as crowded or lively this time like when I visited back at the end of November, but it was nice to be able to hear my friends next to me. McKayla was our server and she was very sweet & attentive to our table of 4. Our drinks were good, especially our favorite Key Lime Martini. We ordered some bbq wings which were great and  the crispy rice smoked salmon was delicious. This spot is such a great vibe will definitely be back soon!

Came here for happy hour the other night with a few friends and loved it! Oddly enough, despite working in the area this was my first time here. The service was great and the food was too! We tried a smattering of things from the happy hour menu as well as some of the main dishes. I would definitely recommend the smoked salmon and rice cake appetizer as well as the chicken tenders, hummus, and fig+ricotta toast. A note, they can make many things gluten free! Also tried the spicy penne vodka and it was great (and actually pretty spicy)! The menu for the main eats is pretty large so I'll definitely be back here. For drinks, they have great cocktails as well as mocktails. Excellent ambience, if not a little loud. Bonus points for the 2010s bangers that came on after happy hour ended! Great for a casual hang!

Great cocktail drinks and appetizers options. We tried the fries, sprouts (a bit honey sweet, i like it), crispy rice with salmon, fish and chips. I really like Fell For a Pornstar coacktail drink, a bit sweet btw. I didnt try any pasta or meal tho so im not sure. They also have a photo booth inside! Great indoor scenery. Friendly staffs. Clean bathroom.

What a fun and eclectic place!  Savanah at the bar killed it with her cocktail skills!  She made great suggestions and with such a lovely Irish accent I knew she knew her craft! The talent Steph and her guitarist were brilliant; great choice of music made me want to stay all night to listen!  The food was great and the staff including a super friendly and knowledgeable Manager, McKenna, made my first visit such a great one, I can't was to go back!

We hosted an engagement dinner at Tara Rose on November 21st, and I cannot say enough amazing things about this restaurant and their team. From the moment we started planning, Steph made everything incredibly easy. She coordinated all the details flawlessly and was so accommodating with our requests and last-minute adjustments.

A huge shout-out to McKenna, who truly went above and beyond. She helped with setup, made sure the bar and dinner areas were perfect, and checked in throughout the night to ensure everything ran smoothly. Her energy, professionalism, and willingness to jump in made a huge difference.

The entire staff was warm, attentive, and extremely professional. The complimentary bottle of Prosecco was such a special touch, and every single guest commented on how delicious the food was, how great the drinks were, and how beautiful the venue is. Several people even mentioned how well they were served and how welcoming the staff felt.

The evening went off without a hitch—truly seamless from start to finish. I’m so grateful to Tara Rose for making this engagement celebration unforgettable. I highly, highly recommend this restaurant for any event or special occasion. Outstanding in every way!
Great drinks, but incredibly loud.
Nice coffee but too many laptops.
Too cramped and loud.

Came here on a whim. Was looking for small plates and local wine. Lucky to have found it. Glad I walked the 18 minutes in 98-degree weather. Place is air conditioned, whew. The employees were so great! Friendly and attentive. All smiles, joking, living.

Had the empanadas. The best I’ve had (but, it’s only my second empanada here in Argentina). Also had the Focaccia Braceada (photo attached). Focaccia is house made and the meat empanadas are as well. The veggie (spinach, blue cheese) is not, still yummy. Cata express (wine tasting) was wonderful: Chardonnay (not oaky at all), a blend (Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, and Malbec), and a Malbec. All excellent.

If you look into the wine window origin (1500s), you may think to support this place; but also, because it has it all: history, modern vibe, good food, good people.
What an elegant little shop. Dispensing great wines and hors d’oeuvres with a smile. Generous portions and reasonable prices, made me feel like I was on the streets of Paris.

We went for an impromptu empanada making and wine tasting. Our host Lourdes provided us with a great experience (in English, gracias) and we all learned a lot. The empanadas were tasty and so was the wine. I would highly recommend this class. The staff were all friendly and they have a nice selection of wine for sale as well. For the Americans it had a very New Orleans vibe.
Great spot for a wine testing (signature and premium options available) which also comes with a small charcuterie board with an empanada !

Gita, romi, and kai were absolutely amazing! They helped me ship 6 bottles of wine to the United States at a very fair price. I spoke no Spanish and they walked me through the process and translated everything for me. Super easy and I got the best wine in Buenos Aires! Thank you!!!
This location is adjacent to the WeWork cowork offices. It is a good place to meet with friends or colleagues. Good coffee and pastries.

First off i wish I could give this place 6 stars. we had their house blend pour over coffee, hands down the best coffee I’ve had in Argentina and one of the best I’ve had on the 4 different continents I’ve been. I bought a few bags to bring back to Macedonia. Then there was the vegan banana bread which is super delicious as well as the vegan apple muffin. We also had the vegan alfajor which is good and the delicious avocado toast. With a really cool style, good music and great baristas this place made our day. Exactly what we were looking for and even better. We went twice while in Buenos Aires and we’ll for sure be back again! This is on my top list of coffee shops. Definitely one of our favorites. Be sure to visit this place. Can’t wait to return and try the cold brew!!

We LOVE this place. Best coffee in Buenos Aires, and the food is amazing. This is the first place we came in BA by luck (we were staying next door) and have been back every day since. Staff are great and so is the music! We go for the caramel macchiato latte and the tea Chemex (for two) sometimes. The coffee infused beer is great too...
It is a nice place. Friendly service, good coffee.
Had a delicious hot chocolate and pastry here for breakfast while I was visiting Buenos Aires. It was quiet and enchanting.
Too mild for my taste. List have no prices and feels they are dynamic. I payed $4829 for a Latte
very kind employees! and i got a delicious cookie!

Wonderful little coffee stand with great quality! The milk is steamed to perfection and blends seamlessly with their selection of coffee beans. César is super friendly and takes the time with each cup of coffee to give you only the best! The cookies are also some of the most decadent I’ve found in the city.
the best cookie and coffee in Buenos Aires! very friendly service. i recommend the Oreo cookie and rush tonic

Upon our arrival, we received a glass of prosecco and an empanadita.  AGUSTÍN provided exceptional service, answered questions, and provided excellent suggestions for our dining experience. We started off with a proprietary tomato over a tomato olive oil puree. We chose a tomato fig tartare which was incredible. The ribeye was good and so big that we had to split it.  Truly the best steak I ever ate.  The French fries were large and hot.  We asked for ketchup but they do not serve ketchup in order to dmsavor the flavor of their own farm vegetables.  We didn’t need ketchup because they were truly awesome
All staff is incredibly attentive and courteous.
The venue is accessible on first floor with accessible tables and at accessible outdoor tables that are covered
The red wine was so good  with the steak.  For dessert, we chose one of their farm-crafted ice cream and what could be better than dulce de leche! Background noise includes conversation.
Truly deserving of one of worlds greatest Restaurant awards

From the moment we arrived until we left 4+ hours later we were pampered and felt like we were family. We arrived at 11:20am for a lunch reservation, as this was the first seating there was a line that had formed. We were given a 1/2 glass of sparkling wine and a beef empanada (simple amazingly tasty) to wait the 10 minutes until we were sat.  We settled in and our 3rd person arrived in time for a tour to see the meats and charcoal fire that they cooked over, they also took us down to see their ample wine  cellar below and then were shown back to our table.  It was a lovely tour and helped connect us to the restaurant and meal without any sales pressure whatsoever.  We started with appetizers of provolone and chorizo before ordering steaks based on “meating” them earlier, I had the strip loin NY and Lisa the tenderloin. Grilled peppers and mash potato sides were ordered. As in most of South America the steaks were lean and flavorful. The tenderloin was very favorable putting the New York and Sirloin steaks at home to shame with little visible fat.

We took more than our time finishing a second bottle of Argentinian wine before desert where the restaurant sent over a couple of other bites to try. Conscious of holding up the table we asked for a quick digestive before leaving, then asked for the check. Before we could leave a glass of dessert wine showed up; “forcing” us to stay longer.  This was our first Michelin star restaurant, and we LOVED IT, while you can get in and out without spending a lot of money; we advise planning on a relaxed time and several courses. The reward will stick with you for a long time.

This little slice of heaven is so perfect for breakfast I wish I could take it home with me. It has a few tables inside and a couple more outside, but with a morning chill that hits harder than adult life, I took shelter indoors. I ordered a ham and cheese croissant that, while not aiming for any Michelin stars, delivered exactly what it promised: simple, satisfying, and just what I needed to rescue my morning. I paired it with an iced coffee — as “special” as it was basic — an ironic combo that somehow lifted my spirits without overthinking it. Service was polite and fast, almost as efficient as the coffee was unpretentious, a charming contradiction of speed and chill vibes. Also they have these modern luxuries we called WiFi and contactless payments. This cozy little coffee shop, with zero restaurant 5-star ambitions, might just be my favorite breakfast spot in Palermo.

I have been to Palermo often and have always intended to make it to Kaldi.  I finally did, and I have been missing out.  The coffee and food were fantastic, and the service (Camila) was outstanding.  Kaldi will now be my go-to spot for coffee and breakfast in the future.

El Preferido in Palermo is a modern, high-end take on a classic Argentine restaurant, offering traditional dishes executed at a very high level. Everything we ordered was excellent, but the standout was the bife de chorizo milanesa — absolutely amazing and perfect for sharing.

The fries were delicious, crunchy on the outside and incredibly soft on the inside, almost like mashed potatoes inside a fry (my first time trying fries like this). The tortilla was served exactly as we ordered it, and the charcuterie plate was also outstanding.

The service was, as expected, on par with the restaurant’s higher price point: attentive, professional, and polished.

Overall, everything was perfect. A top-tier experience and a place well worth visiting.

El Preferido de Palermo is such a popular spot and it’s easy to see why. We were not able to walk in for dinner, so reservations are definitely recommended. We returned a few days later for lunch and were lucky to get a table. The service is polished and attentive, and the ambiance is intimate and romantic. The food was excellent. The Milanese Neapolitan is a standout, perfectly crisp and generously topped, and for dessert the homemade flan or the dulce de leche ice cream are both absolute must-tries.
An excellent, welcoming place with outstanding service and beautiful, modern facilities. I highly recommend it.
I’ve been wanting to come here for a long time! Amazing service, it’s packed because the ambient is beautiful and the food is great! LOVED my coffee!! SOFIA was a great waitress!
Laureano was a really great server! He had great recommendations for me about which Colombian coffee to try. This place is so cute and quaint! Great terrace and we will be back!

A quality specialty coffee shop with a quick stop. They have the biggest and tastiest cookies in the area, and the coffee is always perfectly brewed. A great feature is that you can watch the entire brewing process, and the staff is always very attentive.
If you're the type of person who likes creating content for social media, the staff are very helpful and will gladly film you if you ask.

We went to Don Julio twice during our honeymoon, and overall it was a great experience — with a few mixed notes.

Our first night (and first meal of the honeymoon), we unfortunately waited about an hour past our reservation. That said, the free champagne while waiting was a very nice touch… and I also drank my husband’s, so after an hour of refills I was properly tipsy for our first Don Julio experience 😂🍾. The cheese was incredible. The tomahawk steak was good, but not the best steak we’ve ever had — especially given the hype.

The service and overall experience were still a 10/10. The staff was warm, attentive, and made us feel celebrated. And the ironing!!

Our second visit was honestly better all around — even though we sat outside. Everything felt smoother, better steak, and just hit right.

Would I go back? Absolutely — for the experience, the polenta, the free champs, and the atmosphere. The steak may be a bit over-hyped, but the full Don Julio experience is still very much worth it.

Need to book a table almost 1 month before to get access. Even after booking, we had to wait around 10 mins before seated, but we received a welcome drink and snack for free. Service was good, just maybe would be a good idea to change cutlery after starters, because it was left the same for main course, and for such price restaurant that should be a standard. Waiters could speak better english, as fot foreigner it is quite hard to communicate. Steak was great, and the portion was huge, 500gr of meat, could be posibility to take half portion. Prices are very expensive, but that is a michelin star restaurant

Amazing experience from start to finish. Is this a touristy restaurant? Yes. Is it expensive? That depends on how you define value. Because this is easily one of the best steaks you’ll ever eat.
Like tasting a great wine, take your time with the steak—notice the layers of flavor, the texture, the uniqueness. It’s truly something special.
And the service? Outstanding. The staff makes you feel genuinely at home. Special thanks to Kevin, who went above and beyond and made our night unforgettable. This is a must in BsAs
Great place with kind vibes, amazing service, delicious yougurt bowls and good coffee

Not recommended for turists.

I went into this cafe with my elderly mother because she was very tired and needed a coffee.
I don't speak Spanish but I tried my best to. Right after I asked one of the ladies who worked there about some pastry on the counter, she started laughing, along with the two other ladies. She was struggling to talk because of her laughter. I thought that was weird, but you know, whatever. I sit again and she comes to take our full order. I asked if the tea on the menu was "helado" and she didn't answer with "helado" or "caliente", instead she explained something about the tea, talking very fast, so I assume the answer is "no helado". I tell her "oh, entonces no és helado" and she turns to look at her colleagues and the three laugh again. In fact, they were laughing so much that they forgot to register the cold brew I'd had, and when the older man who I assume was the owner came to get my payment, I could have easily walked away without paying it, if I was a dishonest person.
It's a shame really because the drinks were really good and the owner was polite, but the service was very unpleasant, they need better employees. It was the only bad experience in our whole trip and needless to say I did not tip.
Best croissant ever! Sweet fresh orange juice and delicious coffee. Agustina was super 😊 nice

Excellent service from the moment we walked up to the restaurant. We were greeted warmly and given the option of 3 different seating areas, each of which were beautifully designed. We opted for bar seating so we could see the preparation of the food, which was done with such care and precision that it was stunning to watch. The food was all exceptional… our favorites were the shrimp in garlic and charcoal roasted sole. The cocktails were also exquisite. I especially loved the penecillin, which was made with fresh ginger and came with a beautifully imprinted large ice cube. I also appreciated that when paying with card you are given the option to include tip on the card, rather than needing to carry cash for tip. All around great experience.

This Palermo spot in Buenos Aires is an absolute must-visit—if you can snag a reservation! We got lucky and were seated outside, which made the experience even better. The service was outstanding; the staff even put a sheet over our bags to keep them clean—such a thoughtful touch.
The food is worth every peso. The Milanese steak was perfectly cooked and full of flavor, and the fries were crispy and delicious. Every detail, from service to presentation, felt top-notch.
It’s definitely not cheap, but the experience more than justifies it. Between the amazing food, attentive staff, and great atmosphere, this place is hard to beat. We’d come back in a heartbeat and highly recommend it to anyone looking for an unforgettable meal in Buenos Aires.

Absolute pleasure. Strolled in casually on a Monday evening, got a table outside instantly (solo). The food was well above average if not exceptional bistro style. Not that expensive if selective and skip wine. Steak was very tender, I think the meat is dry aged as standard. Dulce de leche helado was cheap and cheerful. Would recommend any time!
Delicious matcha latte! The matcha was strong and great quality and the ice ratio was perfect. Want to go again already!

How lovely to find places like this! A warm, peaceful, and incredibly pleasant atmosphere. The coffee was amazing, and the food was delicious—the kind you truly savor. Plus, the service was excellent, and the whole place has such a lovely, welcoming energy ❤️ Definitely a place to return to 🤎

One of our favorite places for breakfast whenever we’re in the area. The coffee is absolutely delicious, and the food is amazing every time!
Lucia was wonderful - so friendly, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. She made our morning even more special.
Such a great café with a lovely atmosphere. We’ll definitely be back!

My first time in Argentina and I would come back here for brunch ANY day! Super super good. Karen was a wonderful waitress! Gracias Karen! Very attentive and had great recommendations. I said YES to all of them and I was happy with all of them.  I highly recommend coming here!
Great place to have delicious breakfast and work a bit on your laptop. The service is fast and friendly. Avo toast is super delicious and coffe good as well

We stayed in Palermo and we stopped to have breakfast at this restaurant. They have great service, pastries and coffee. It was difficult to find eggs for breakfast in BA but we glad we found this place. The avocado toast was delicious. They also have fresh orange juice and keto and vegetarian options. They have a sitting area outside. In general a great place for a good breakfast in Palermo.
We had a late lunch. Affogato and tuna salad were really good. The homemade lemonade was tasteless. The staff was kind, but extremely slow, but this is the Latino style
The food and staff was great. We received menus without the prices, which I think is questionable. Very typical instafriendly place.

I loved everything about this place. The atmosphere is amazing, the decor is great, the people are extremely kind, and very helpful. From the possible  owner, to possibly the manager, and their bartender. Everyone was exceptional including the cook although I did not catch his name. He did an incredible job on the bacon cheeseburger honestly. He asked me if I liked it so he must have been doing something new and I can tell him this, IT WORKS!. This place will be recommended to anyone I know that travels to Buenos Aires!
You could easily get out of here for less than 30k for one person.
Fantastic bar, really cool atmosphere and welcoming staff. Drinks were delicious!! Had a great seat watching the bar tender make the cocktails.

We were walking by the Cic Bar and saw the sandwich boards announcing their  happy hour specials. We turned right around and ordered the pisco sour special and the malbec special. Along with the drinks, the kind waitress brought us out French fries, salted peanuts, a shot of apricot vodka with a meringue topping and a sweet potato dessert of some kind - ALL on the house! We sat outside and enjoyed the sunshine. I highly recommend this restaurant.

Went to this place for a late lunch and they promptly seated us! Thank you for that! They sent  bread and champagne to the table, seemed like it would be complimentary and a nice touch - but they charge for it. We ordered a tropical mango juice that had no flavor, no taste of mango whatsoever. For the main course, ordered 4 dishes, the white pasta had no flavor at all, no salt, no pepper, no cheese, and way too bland, the tomato sauce, no flavor either, just red in color. The same with all the food that we ordered, the pasta was thick and raw in some cases. The most flavorful food items at the table were the olive oil and the balsamic vinegar at the table. So if you do like bland food, you will likely be ok with it and enjoy it. We did not.

Lovely place and great service we received from Italo. We had such a good time in here.

Tiramisu was huge so I recommend sharing with friends or family.  Latte was excellent!

Complementary sparkling wine and brownies were total surprise.  Highly recommend Ciro!

Muy bien.  These people are great.  There wasn’t many people in there tonight but they deserved more on a Saturday night.  A lot of effort went into the food and drinks and they were very reasonably priced.  Leave Palermo and come out and support this place.

My favorite bar in Buenos Aires so far! I’ve been here for three weeks and have never experienced such amazing customer service. The bartenders were personable and helpful (even with my broken Spanish) and the waitress was absolutely lovely. Could not recommend any higher!
